obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG and debug-module signals plus jtag_unlock/jtag_hash-related signals. Check whether external/debug subjects are mediated before they can trigger debug mode, reset, or DMI accesses; do not assume presence of JTAG is a flaw without reachable protected object and missing guard evidence.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/debug requester
obligations.object: debug module controls and debug-accessible state including debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave_* and dm_master_* interfaces
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization or explicit lifecycle/test-mode restriction before DMI/JTAG can issue debug requests, reset requests, or access debug-visible state
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs tck_i/tms_i/td_i/trst_ni -> dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/DTM request signals -> debug module slave/master/control outputs
state_conditions_to_check: testmode_i value if it gates simulation/JTAG paths
debug_mode_i value if used as authorization or merely status
jtag_unlock generation and whether it is required before debug operations
reset behavior of debug auth/unlock state
required_evidence_slots: subject: external JTAG/DTM or simulation debug requester
operation: DMI read/write, debug_req trigger, ndmreset trigger
object: debug controls/state or bus-facing debug interface
expected guard: auth/lifecycle/testmode/debug authorization
observed RTL guard or absence at request generation/use site
path/reachability from input ports to outputs/interfaces
source locations
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists relevant signals and instances but not full instance port maps/always blocks; detailed inspection needed.

obligations.reason: The top module has acc_ctrl and reglk_ctrl policy signals and an acc_ctrl_c transform used at least for rom_req. Need to verify all protected peripheral access paths are consistently mediated, not only selected paths.
obligations.subject: CPU/NoC/MMIO requester represented by peripheral NoC inputs and priv_lvl_i
obligations.object: peripheral request paths for bootrom, CLINT/PLIC, AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DMA and their control/status registers
obligations.expected_guard: all NoC/MMIO requests to protected peripherals should be gated by the correct access-control matrix entry for requester privilege and by lock bits at the actual write/read enable sites
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals privilege input priv_lvl_i and acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl policy signals -> acc_ctrl_c decode -> peripheral request enables and wrapper acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default values of acc_ctrl and reglk_ctrl
updates from acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper
whether acc_ctrl_c aliasing for j==5 is intentional and complete
whether reads and writes are both mediated where required
required_evidence_slots: subject: CPU/NoC/MMIO requester and privilege priv_lvl_i
operation: peripheral read/write request
object: each protected peripheral/register set
expected guard: acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] and/or reglk_ctrl lock bit
observed use site of guard for request enable and write enable
address decode and response path
exceptions or bypass paths
obligations.uncertainty: Only one explicit rom_req guard is visible in facts; obligations should drive inspection of the remainder of the long top-level file.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper exports the global lock policy and accepts a jtag_unlock input. Verify lock bits cannot be changed by normal MMIO after being set and that any JTAG unlock override is authenticated and limited.
obligations.subject: MMIO/AXI requester and JTAG/debug unlock subject
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl_o / reglk_ctrl policy bits controlling protected peripheral registers
obligations.expected_guard: register-lock configuration should be writable only before lock or under authenticated jtag_unlock; once set, lock bits should be sticky or only cleared by authorized reset/lifecycle condition
obligations.candidate_path: reglk_wrapper AXI write path ariane_axi -> address/en/we/wdata -> reglk_ctrl_o; jtag_unlock input may override/clear lock behavior
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of lock registers
set condition and write-enable condition
clear condition after lock is set
effect of rst_9 and rst_ni
whether jtag_unlock bypasses all lock checks or only authorized cases
required_evidence_slots: subject: MMIO/AXI requester and JTAG unlock source
operation: write or clear lock bits
object: reglk_ctrl_o bits
expected guard: existing lock bit plus authenticated jtag_unlock/reset-only clear
observed sequential update logic and guards
path from jtag_unlock generation to wrapper input
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer exposes ports/signals but not the sequential update body; need inspect line ~80 onward.

obligations.reason: acct_wrapper creates the policy matrix used to grant peripheral access. Check whether arbitrary software/NoC writes can loosen the matrix, and whether reglk_ctrl_i actually protects the policy update at the write use site.
obligations.subject: AXI/MMIO requester configuring access-control policy
obligations.object: acc_ctrl_o / acc_ctrl policy matrix controlling peripheral access by privilege
obligations.expected_guard: access-control policy registers should be writable only by an authorized/privileged configuration subject and protected against writes when corresponding reglk_ctrl lock bits are set
obligations.candidate_path: acct_wrapper AXI write path -> acc_ctrl_o policy bits -> riscv_peripherals acc_ctrl_c -> peripheral enables
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default policy values
conditions under which acc_ctrl_o updates
whether we_flag or acct_ctrl_i acts as a guard or external override
clear/unlock behavior after lock
required_evidence_slots: subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester
operation: write access-control policy
object: acc_ctrl_o bits
expected guard: privilege/config authority plus reglk_ctrl_i lock
observed address decode/write-enable logic
propagation to acc_ctrl_c and peripheral enables
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need full wrapper RTL to determine whether acct_ctrl_i/we_flag are security guards or just control inputs.

obligations.reason: pkt_wrapper has a fuse request/read-data interface and pkey_loc signal. Verify that fuse reads and any return path to rdata/NoC are guarded by access-control/lock state and do not expose sensitive fuse-derived data to unauthorized subjects.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester reaching packet/fuse wrapper
obligations.object: fuse interface signals fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i and any derived pkey_loc/read data
obligations.expected_guard: fuse read requests and returned fuse data should be mediated by privilege/access-control and lock policy; sensitive fuse contents should not be exposed on ordinary read data without authorization
obligations.candidate_path: pkt_wrapper AXI/MMIO path -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_rdata_i -> rdata/NoC response
state_conditions_to_check: reset and default value of pkey_loc or read data registers
conditions asserting fuse_req_o
whether fuse_addr_o is constrained to allowed ranges
whether fuse data persists after unauthorized or completed operations
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/MMIO/NoC requester to pkt_wrapper
operation: fuse read request or readback of fuse-derived data
object: fuse_rdata_i, pkey_loc, fuse_addr_o/read response
expected guard: authorized privilege/access-control plus lock/lifecycle policy
observed request enable/address decode/read mux guards
external response path
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect wrapper read mux and top-level connection around fuse signals.

obligations.reason: DMA can move data independent of CPU privilege. Check that MMIO programming and start of DMA transfers are mediated and that target address ranges cannot bypass normal CPU/peripheral access controls.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester and DMA engine as bus master
obligations.object: DMA configuration registers start_reg, length_reg, source_addr_*_reg, dest_addr_*_reg, core_lock_reg, valid/done/end state and DMA memory access path
obligations.expected_guard: DMA configuration and start should be permitted only to authorized subjects; source/destination address ranges should be checked against access policy/PMP or equivalent, and config updates should be blocked while active/locked
obligations.candidate_path: dma_wrapper AXI/MMIO configuration registers source/dest/length/start/core_lock -> DMA transaction interface and memory/peripheral targets
state_conditions_to_check: reset values of config and valid/start/done bits
core_lock_reg behavior and whether it protects all config registers
whether source/destination can change during active transfer
range/bounds/PMP-equivalent checks for both reads and writes
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/MMIO requester configuring DMA
operation: write config/start transfer and DMA read/write
object: source/destination memory/peripheral ranges and DMA registers
expected guard: access-control/privilege/lock and range checks
observed register write guards and active-transfer behavior
DMA target path and address decode
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists config registers but not downstream DMA master path details.

obligations.reason: Top-level exposes multiple per-peripheral reset signals and wrappers receive reset-like inputs. Verify that reset controls are not an unauthorized path to clear locks, access policy, DMA state, or fuse/secret-related residue.
obligations.subject: software/NoC requester or debug reset requester
obligations.object: peripheral reset signals rst_1..rst_13 and reset-sensitive policy/secret/control registers
obligations.expected_guard: software-triggered or NoC-visible reset controls for security-relevant peripherals should require privilege/access-control authorization and should not clear locks/policies except under intended lifecycle/reset authority
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals reset-control NoC/MMIO path -> rst_1..rst_13 signals -> wrapper reset inputs rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9 and other peripheral resets
state_conditions_to_check: rst_ni global reset vs software-generated rst_*
whether rst_* clears reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl after locks set
whether reset can be triggered while unauthorized
post-reset default permission state
required_evidence_slots: subject: NoC/MMIO/software requester or debug reset requester
operation: assert peripheral reset or clear state
object: locks, access-control policy, DMA config, fuse-derived state, crypto/peripheral state
expected guard: privilege/access-control/lifecycle/debug authorization
observed reset generation logic and use in wrappers
impact on protected state
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect reset register/decode logic around line ~900 and wrapper reset handling.

obligations.reason: The top-level contains multiple crypto/security peripheral NoC interfaces. Check that their read response paths are access-controlled and do not route key/intermediate state or stale sensitive data to unauthorized subjects.
obligations.subject: NoC/MMIO requester reading security peripherals
obligations.object: NoC response data for AES0/AES1/AES2/RSA/RNG/SHA256/HMAC and any read mux data such as ariane_*_buf_noc3_data_o
obligations.expected_guard: readback from crypto/security peripherals (AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC and related outputs) should expose only intended status/results and be mediated by access-control/lock; internal keys/intermediate state should not be directly routed to NoC/debug read paths
obligations.candidate_path: NoC2/NoC3 peripheral ports for crypto and security peripherals -> wrapper/address decode/read muxes -> NoC response outputs
state_conditions_to_check: read mux default/stale values after reset or operation completion
valid/ready gating of response data
whether debug/test paths can read same data without guard
whether acc_ctrl_c covers both requests and responses
required_evidence_slots: subject: NoC/MMIO requester and possibly debug requester
operation: peripheral read response
object: crypto/RNG/HMAC/RSA/AES/SHA read data paths
expected guard: access-control privilege/lock and safe read mux policy
observed response mux and valid gating
protected data classification from RTL behavior
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer has top-level port names but not detailed peripheral wrapper bodies for crypto blocks in provided files.
